questionText: The following pie-charts show the distribution of students of graduate and post-graduate levels in 7 different Institutes: M, N, P, Q, R, S and T in a town. Total Number of Students of Graduate Level = 27300 and Total Number of Students of Post-Graduate Level = 24700. How many students of institutes M and S are studying at graduate level? (Institute M = 17% of graduate level; Institute S = 14% of graduate level)

steps.explanation: Find the number of students at graduate level in institute M.
Find the number of students at graduate level in institute S.
Add the two values to get the total.

solution.commonMistake: Using the post-graduate total (24700) instead of the graduate total (27300).

questionText: The following pie-charts show the distribution of students of graduate and post-graduate levels in 7 different Institutes: M, N, P, Q, R, S and T in a town. Total Number of Students of Graduate Level = 27300 and Total Number of Students of Post-Graduate Level = 24700. Total number of students studying at post-graduate level from institutes N and P is: (Institute N = 15% of post-graduate level; Institute P = 12% of post-graduate level)

steps.explanation: Find the number of students at post-graduate level in institute N.
Find the number of students at post-graduate level in institute P.
Add the two values.

solution.commonMistake: Using the graduate total (27300) instead of post-graduate total (24700).

questionText: The following pie-charts show the distribution of students of graduate and post-graduate levels in 7 different Institutes: M, N, P, Q, R, S and T in a town. Total Number of Students of Graduate Level = 27300 and Total Number of Students of Post-Graduate Level = 24700. What is the total number of graduate and post-graduate level students in institute R? (Institute R = 18% of graduate level; Institute R = 14% of post-graduate level)

steps.explanation: Find the number of graduate level students in institute R.
Find the number of post-graduate level students in institute R.
Add both values to find the total students in institute R.

solution.commonMistake: Adding percentages first and applying to a single total instead of applying each percentage to its respective total.

questionText: The following pie-charts show the distribution of students of graduate and post-graduate levels in 7 different Institutes: M, N, P, Q, R, S and T in a town. Total Number of Students of Graduate Level = 27300 and Total Number of Students of Post-Graduate Level = 24700. What is the ratio between the number of students studying at post-graduate and graduate levels respectively from institute S? (Institute S = 21% of post-graduate level; Institute S = 14% of graduate level)

steps.explanation: Find the number of post-graduate students in institute S.
Find the number of graduate students in institute S.
Compute the ratio PG : Graduate.

solution.commonMistake: Reversing the ratio (graduate to post-graduate instead of post-graduate to graduate).

modelName: Pie Chart - Ratio Between Two Sectors

questionText: The following pie-charts show the distribution of students of graduate and post-graduate levels in 7 different Institutes: M, N, P, Q, R, S and T in a town. Total Number of Students of Graduate Level = 27300 and Total Number of Students of Post-Graduate Level = 24700. What is the ratio between the number of students studying at post-graduate level from institute S and the number of students studying at graduate level from institute Q? (Institute S = 21% of post-graduate level; Institute Q = 13% of graduate level)

steps.explanation: Find the number of post-graduate students in institute S.
Find the number of graduate students in institute Q.
Compute the ratio S(PG) : Q(Graduate).

solution.commonMistake: Mixing up which institute's percentage belongs to which total (graduate vs post-graduate).

modelName: Pie Chart - Ratio Between Two Sectors

questionText: The following pie-diagram shows the percentage composition of the human body. Skin = $\frac{1}{10}$ of total body, Bones = $\frac{1}{6}$ of total body. In the human body, what part is made of neither bones nor skin?

steps.explanation: Identify the fractions for bones and skin from the pie chart.
Add the fractions for bones and skin.
Subtract from 1 to find the fraction made of neither bones nor skin.
Compare 11/15 with the given options. None of the options (1/40, 3/80, 2/5) match 11/15.

solution.shortcut: Sum bones + skin = 1/6 + 1/10 = 4/15; remainder = 11/15, which matches none of the given fractions.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting to subtract the sum from 1, or making an arithmetic error in finding LCM.

questionText: The following pie-diagram shows the percentage composition of the human body. Proteins in muscles = $\frac{1}{3}$ of total proteins; Proteins in bones = $\frac{1}{6}$ of total proteins (i.e., proteins are $\frac{1}{6}$ of bones). What is the ratio of the distribution of proteins in the muscles to that of the distribution of proteins in the bones?

steps.explanation: From the pie chart, muscles constitute 1/3 of the body and proteins constitute 1/6 of muscles.
Bones constitute 1/6 of the body and proteins constitute 1/6 of bones.
Compute the ratio proteins in muscles to proteins in bones.

solution.commonMistake: Inverting the ratio, giving 1:2 instead of 2:1.

formulas.title: Protein fraction
formulas.expression: \text{Protein in part} = \frac{\text{protein fraction of part}}{1} \times \text{part fraction of whole}

questionText: The following pie-diagram shows the percentage composition of the human body where water = 70% of total body weight. What will be the quantity of water in the body of a person weighing 50 kg?

steps.explanation: From the pie chart, water constitutes 70% of the total body weight.

solution.commonMistake: Using wrong percentage for water from the chart.

questionText: The following pie-diagram shows the percentage composition of the human body. Skin = $\frac{1}{10}$ of total body; proteins constitute 16% of skin. What percent of the total weight of human body is equivalent to the weight of the proteins in skin in human body?

steps.explanation: Let the total body weight be x kg. Skin constitutes 1/10 of the body.
Proteins constitute 16% of the skin.
Express as a percentage of the total body weight x.

solution.shortcut: Protein in skin = 16% × (1/10) of total = 1.6% of total.
solution.commonMistake: Forgetting to express as percentage of the whole body (not just skin).

modelName: Pie Chart - Sub-component Percentage of Whole

formulas.title: Sub-component % of whole
formulas.expression: \% = \frac{\text{component fraction of part} \times \text{part fraction of whole}}{1} \times 100

questionText: The following pie-diagram shows the percentage composition of the human body where proteins = 16% and other dry elements = 14% of total body. To show the distribution of proteins and other dry elements in the human body, the arc of the circle should subtend at the centre an angle of

steps.explanation: Find the total percentage of proteins and other dry elements combined.
Convert the percentage to the central angle in the pie chart.

solution.commonMistake: Applying 360 to each component separately without adding the percentages first.
